# Nuntia - Complete Documentation

> Nuntia is a TypeScript application that connects Telegram and WhatsApp to OpenCode's multi-agent AI coding system. Users send natural language coding requests via chat and receive real-time streaming output, automatic Git branching, and TDD workflow enforcement.

Source: https://github.com/wngfra/Nuntia
License: Apache 2.0
Runtime: Node.js 20+ / TypeScript 5+ (ESM)

---

# Getting Started

## Prerequisites

| Requirement | Version | Notes |
|-------------|---------|-------|
| Node.js | 20+ | ESM modules required |
| OpenCode CLI | latest | Must be on PATH |
| Telegram Bot Token | - | From @BotFather |

## Installation

```bash
git clone https://github.com/wngfra/Nuntia.git
cd Nuntia
npm install
```

## Configuration

Copy the example environment file and fill in the required values:

```bash
cp .env.example .env
```

Three variables are required to start:

```dotenv
TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=123456:ABC-DEF1234...
ALLOWED_CHAT_IDS=123456789
MASTER_KEY=a1b2c3d4...  # Generate with: openssl rand -hex 32
```

## Running

### Development

```bash
npm run dev
```

Uses tsx for on-the-fly TypeScript execution.

### Production

```bash
npm run build
npm start
```

### Docker

```bash
cp .env.example .env
docker compose up -d
```

## First Interaction

Send `/start` to your bot on Telegram. Then send a natural language coding request:

```
Build a simple Express.js hello world server with a health check endpoint
```

The bot will:
1. Prepend the TDD workflow system prompt
2. Spawn OpenCode in your project directory
3. Stream agent stage transitions to chat in real-time
4. Auto-commit the result to a new Git branch
5. Send a summary with files changed and branch name

Each chat maps to an isolated project directory:

```
workspaces/
└── tg_123456789/
    └── default/
        ├── uploads/
        ├── .git/
        ├── SPEC.md
        ├── tests/
        ├── src/
        ├── README.md
        └── CHANGELOG.md
```

---

# Configuration

All configuration is via environment variables loaded from a `.env` file.

## Required Variables

| Variable | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN | Telegram Bot API token from @BotFather |
| ALLOWED_CHAT_IDS | Comma-separated whitelist of chat IDs |
| MASTER_KEY | 32-byte hex string for AES-256-GCM encryption |

## Optional Variables

### Transport

| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| WHATSAPP_ENABLED | false | Enable the WhatsApp adapter |

### Workspace

| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| WORKSPACE_ROOT | ./workspaces | Root directory for all project workspaces |
| OPENCODE_BIN | opencode | Path to the OpenCode CLI binary |
| TASK_TIMEOUT_MS | 600000 | Maximum task execution time (10 minutes) |

### Model

| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| DEFAULT_MODEL | claude-sonnet-4-20250514 | Default OpenCode model |

### Git

| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| GIT_REMOTE_URL | (empty) | Remote URL for /push |
| GIT_USER_NAME | Nuntia | Git commit author name |
| GIT_USER_EMAIL | nuntia@localhost | Git commit author email |

### Files

| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| MAX_UPLOAD_SIZE_MB | 50 | Maximum file upload size in MB |

### Logging

| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| LOG_LEVEL | info | Pino log level |

## Directory Structure

```
WORKSPACE_ROOT/
├── tg_<user_chat_id>/          # Telegram DM
│   ├── default/                # Default project
│   └── my-api/                 # Named project (via /new)
├── tg_group_<group_id>/        # Telegram group
│   └── default/
├── wa_<chat_jid>/              # WhatsApp chat
│   └── default/
└── .nuntia/                    # Internal state
    ├── sessions.json
    ├── keystore.json
    └── whatsapp-auth/
```

## WhatsApp Setup

When WHATSAPP_ENABLED=true, the bot prints a QR code to the terminal on first start. Scan with WhatsApp to authenticate. Auth state is persisted.

## Chat ID Whitelist

ALLOWED_CHAT_IDS accepts user chat IDs, group chat IDs, and individual user IDs (checked as fallback). Messages from non-whitelisted chats are silently ignored.

---

# Bot Commands

All commands work on both Telegram and WhatsApp unless noted otherwise.

## Quick Reference

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| /start | Initialize bot and create default project |
| /help | Show command list |
| /new <name> | Create and switch to a new project |
| /switch <name> | Switch active project |
| /projects | List all projects |
| /files | List project files (tree, depth 2) |
| /cat <path> | View file contents |
| /status | Show session info |
| /model <name> | Switch OpenCode model |
| /models | List available models |
| /apikey <provider> <key> | Store an API key (encrypted) |
| /apikey list | Show configured providers |
| /cancel | Cancel running task |
| /clear | Clear session state |
| /branches | List Git branches |
| /checkout <branch> | Switch Git branch |
| /push | Push branch to remote |
| /diff | Show uncommitted or last-commit diff |
| /uploads | List uploaded files |
| /rm <path> | Delete a file |

## Group Chat Behavior

In Telegram groups:
- All members on the whitelist can send prompts
- Admin-only commands: /model, /apikey, /new, /switch
- User attribution: each message includes [User: Name] in the prompt
- Task queue: concurrent requests queued sequentially
- Reply threading: bot responds as a reply to the requesting message

---

# File Uploads

Nuntia supports file uploads from Telegram and WhatsApp. Files are saved to the active project directory.

## Supported File Types

Any file type: images (PNG, JPG, SVG), documents (PDF, TXT, MD), code files (TS, JS, PY), archives (ZIP, TAR.GZ), data files (JSON, CSV, SQL).

## How It Works

- Upload without caption: file saved, no task triggered
- Upload with caption: file saved, caption sent to OpenCode with file reference
- Reference uploaded files in later prompts by path

## File Naming

Filenames are sanitized (.. stripped, slashes stripped, leading dots stripped). Duplicates get numeric suffixes (design_2.png, design_3.png).

## Size Limits

Default 50MB limit (MAX_UPLOAD_SIZE_MB). Files exceeding the limit are rejected.

---

# Git Workflow

Nuntia automatically manages Git repositories for each project.

## Auto-Initialization

New projects get: git init, user.name/email config, .gitignore, initial commit on main.

## Task Branching

On task completion:
1. Creates new branch from HEAD
2. Stages all changes (git add -A)
3. Commits with message "[Nuntia] <task description>"
4. Reports branch name and changed files to chat

Branch names: first ~40 chars of prompt, kebab-cased, with YYYYMMDD-HHmmss timestamp.

## Git Commands

- /branches: list all local branches
- /checkout <branch>: switch branch
- /diff: show uncommitted changes (or last commit diff)
- /push: push to configured remote (GIT_REMOTE_URL)

## Non-Blocking Design

Git failures don't prevent task output delivery.

---

# Architecture

## System Diagram

```
Telegram/WhatsApp Users
        |
Transport Layer (grammY / Baileys)
        |
Session Manager (chat ID -> project dir, task queue)
        |
    +---+---+
    |       |
Command   Task Pipeline
Router    (Workflow Prompt -> OpenCode Runner -> Output Parser -> Formatter)
    |           |
    +---+---+---+
        |   |   |
    Git  File  Crypto
    Mgr  Hdlr  Store
```

## Module Responsibilities

- Transport Layer: abstracts Telegram/WhatsApp behind TransportAdapter interface
- Session Manager: maps chats to persistent sessions and project directories
- OpenCode Runner: spawns CLI subprocess, streams stdout/stderr, handles timeout
- Formatters: platform-specific output (Telegram MarkdownV2, WhatsApp plain text)
- Git Manager: auto-init repos, task branches, commits, push, diff via simple-git
- File Handler: uploads with dedup, sanitization, path traversal prevention
- Crypto Keystore: AES-256-GCM encryption for API keys
- Command Router: dispatches /commands to handler modules

## Data Flow: Task Execution

1. Transport adapter receives and converts message
2. Session manager authorizes via whitelist
3. File upload saved if attachment present
4. Workflow system prompt prepended
5. OpenCode CLI spawned in project directory
6. stdout parsed and streamed to chat
7. Platform formatter prepares messages
8. On completion, commit to new task branch
9. Summary sent with branch, files, test results

## Concurrency Model

- One OpenCode process per session
- Task queue per chat
- Transport-level throttling (2s Telegram, 3s WhatsApp)
- Non-blocking Git
- Graceful shutdown via SIGTERM

---

# Security

## API Key Protection

- AES-256-GCM encryption with random 16-byte IV per entry
- Telegram: /apikey message auto-deleted after processing
- Keys never logged, echoed, or stored in plaintext

## Access Control

- ALLOWED_CHAT_IDS whitelist (silent ignore for non-whitelisted)
- Admin-only commands in groups: /model, /apikey, /new, /switch

## File Upload Security

- Path traversal prevention (../ stripped, slashes stripped)
- Resolved path validated within project directory
- Size limits enforced before download

## Process Isolation

- Each OpenCode task runs as child process with cwd set to project directory
- Configurable timeout with SIGTERM -> SIGKILL escalation
- Docker deployment recommended for additional isolation

## Network Exposure

- No HTTP endpoints (Telegram uses long polling)
- WhatsApp connection is outbound-only WebSocket

---

# API Reference - Module Map

| Module | File | Key Exports |
|--------|------|-------------|
| Config | src/config.ts | AppConfig, loadConfig(), resetConfig() |
| Logger | src/logger.ts | rootLogger, createLogger() |
| Utils | src/utils.ts | checkDiskSpace() |
| Transport Types | src/transport/types.ts | Platform, IncomingMessage, TransportAdapter, MessageHandler |
| Telegram Adapter | src/transport/telegram.ts | TelegramAdapter |
| WhatsApp Adapter | src/transport/whatsapp.ts | WhatsAppAdapter |
| Session Store | src/session/store.ts | SessionData, SessionStore |
| Session Manager | src/session/manager.ts | QueuedRequest, SessionManager |
| Output Parser | src/runner/parser.ts | AgentStage, ParsedLine, OutputStreamParser, stripAnsi(), parseLine() |
| Workflow Prompt | src/runner/workflow-prompt.ts | WORKFLOW_SYSTEM_PROMPT, buildPrompt(), buildFileReferencePrompt() |
| OpenCode Runner | src/runner/opencode.ts | TaskOptions, TaskResult, RunnerEvent, OpenCodeRunner |
| Crypto Keystore | src/crypto/keystore.ts | encrypt(), decrypt(), storeApiKey(), getApiKey(), listProviders() |
| Git Manager | src/git/manager.ts | deriveBranchName(), GitManager |
| File Handler | src/files/handler.ts | sanitizeFilename(), deduplicateFilename(), FileHandler |
| Formatters | src/formatter/*.ts | escapeMarkdownV2(), formatStageTransition(), formatCodeBlock(), formatTaskSummary() |
| Command Router | src/commands/index.ts | CommandContext, CommandRouter |
| Command Modules | src/commands/*.ts | ProjectCommands, ModelCommands, SessionCommands, GitCommands, FileCommands |

## Export Summary

| Category | Count |
|----------|-------|
| Interfaces | 13 |
| Types | 4 |
| Classes | 14 |
| Functions | 21 |
| Constants | 2 |
| Source files | 23 |

---

# Contributing

## Development Setup

```bash
git clone https://github.com/wngfra/Nuntia.git
cd Nuntia
npm install
```

### Scripts

| Script | Command | Description |
|--------|---------|-------------|
| npm run dev | tsx src/index.ts | Development mode |
| npm run build | tsc | Compile TypeScript |
| npm start | node dist/index.js | Production |
| npm test | vitest run | Run all tests |
| npm run test:watch | vitest | Watch mode |
| npm run lint | tsc --noEmit | Type-check |

### Tech Stack

| Layer | Technology |
|-------|-----------|
| Runtime | Node.js 20+ / TypeScript 5+ (ESM) |
| Telegram | grammY |
| WhatsApp | @whiskeysockets/baileys |
| Git | simple-git |
| Encryption | Node crypto (AES-256-GCM) |
| Logging | pino |
| Testing | vitest |
| Docs | VitePress |

## Adding a Transport

1. Implement TransportAdapter interface in src/transport/newtransport.ts
2. Update Platform type in src/transport/types.ts
3. Convert platform messages to IncomingMessage
4. Add rate limiting appropriate for the platform
5. Register in src/index.ts entry point
6. Optionally add a formatter in src/formatter/
7. Add config variables in src/config.ts

## Adding Commands

Option 1: Add handler method to existing command module, wire in router, add to help text.
Option 2: Create new command module class, register in router, add admin restriction if needed.

Handler pattern: async handleX(msg: IncomingMessage, transport: TransportAdapter, args?: string): Promise<void>

## Testing

Vitest test suite: 79 tests across 6 files covering parser, crypto, session, git, files, and runner modules.

Tests set their own environment variables. Config module caches results - call resetConfig() after env var changes.

Patterns: temp directories for file system tests, direct testing for pure functions, env setup for classes with dependencies.
